source_pr.title: executor: refactor merge join
source_pr.body:
1. split `readerIterator` to `mergeJoinInnerTable` and `mergeJoinOuterTable`, use `mergeJoinInnerTable` to returns all the inner rows within the same join group, use `mergeJoinOuterTable` to return a Chunk of outer rows and iterate on it when we do the merge join process.
`(rather than buffer all the outer rows with the same join key, which can also reduce the memory usage during merge join)`

2. fix a potential bug caused by always appending the unmatched outer row to the very beginning output Chunk. `(caused by readerIterator.nextSelectedRow() )`,
this can also promise the result order according to the output rows.

symptoms: tk.MustQuery("explain select /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t2) */ * from t1 left outer join t2 on t1.a=t2.a and t1.a!=3 order by t1.a;").Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/join_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery("explain select /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t2) */ * from t1 left outer join t2 on t1.a=t2.a and t1.a!=3 order by t1.a;").Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/join_test.go)
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery("explain select /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t2) */ * from t1 left outer join t2 on t1.a=t2.a and t1.a!=3 order by t1.a;").Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/join_test.go)
files: executor/builder.go, executor/executor.go, executor/join_test.go, executor/merge_join.go, executor/sort.go
